item.title: EQT makes improved £9.7bn offer for Intertek
item.description: Previous bid for FTSE 100 group from Swedish private equity firm rejected

item.title: EQT warns AI fears will stall sales of private equity software stakes
item.description: Investor concerns that technology could hit companies’ business models will derail exits, says Swedish group

item.title: Switzerland hits UBS with proposed $20bn capital increase
item.description: Federal Council sets out plans for banking reform after months of lobbying by country’s biggest lender

item.title: UK lenders urge judges to block mass motor finance lawsuits
item.description: Case in Court of Appeal will help shape whether motorists can make claims outside of £9.1bn redress scheme

item.title: Ukraine miner Ferrexpo warns it could run out of cash by August
item.description: London-listed company’s shares plunge as it considers capital raise of at least $100mn

item.title: Eni founder’s heir clashes with Meloni government over name use
item.description: Italian government’s ‘Mattei plan’ for Africa is part of Rome’s efforts to curb migration

item.title: Boeing regains delivery lead over Airbus as revenues jump
item.description: US aircraft maker narrows losses and posts widest gap over European rival since 2018

item.title: Apple controls the tech sector’s Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It may have stumbled in the AI race but the company’s new CEO will find it still has distinct advantages

item.title: House prices in London’s wealthiest boroughs suffer double-digit decline
item.description: Westminster and Kensington and Chelsea property valuations sink close to 2013 levels

item.title: UK dilutes post-crisis ‘fit and proper’ rules for financial services staff
item.description: Changes are first stage in wider shake-up by Financial Conduct Authority

item.title: Balls, boats and billionaires: Como 1907’s lifestyle brand aspirations
item.description: Once, Como 1907 was just a football club. Now, its owners are trying to build a luxury ecosystem around its glamorous location

item.title: Who will pay the rising costs of disaster insurance?
item.description: Governments are grappling with a fiendish policy dilemma

item.title: Sam’s Club launches one-hour US delivery with shoppers in ‘on-demand phase’
item.description: Walmart-owned warehouse chain will compete for market share from rivals Amazon and Costco

item.title: France and Germany extend deadline to try to save joint fighter jet
item.description: Mediators have until April 28 to overcome power struggle between Airbus and Dassault Aviation

item.title: Tui cuts profit outlook as Iran war makes holidaymakers cautious
item.description: Travel operator suspends revenue guidance and says customers are increasingly booking at the last minute

item.title: What does AI really mean for your work? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Sarah O’Connor, John Burn Murdoch and Madhumita Murgia on Thursday April 23 at 1pm BST

item.title: Moscow to suspend Kazakh oil flows through key pipeline supplying Berlin
item.description: Russian plan puts majority of German city’s supplies of petrol, kerosene and heating fuel at risk

item.title: Dettol maker Reckitt hit by Russia sanctions and weak Europe demand
item.description: UK-listed group no longer permitted to ship cleaning products to Russia under changes to EU sanctions

item.title: The remarkable renaissance of high-frequency traders
item.description: Money, it’s a gas. Grab that cash with both hands and make a stash

item.title: War disruption forces world’s biggest condom maker to raise prices by up to 30%
item.description: Malaysia’s Karex says it has to pass on higher cost of raw materials to customers

item.title: FirstFT: Trump extends Iran ceasefire
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Deutsche Telekom weighs merger and Nestlé’s China business

item.title: Reebok plans hundreds of China stores to capture activewear boom
item.description: Owner Authentic Brands hopes bricks-and-mortar relaunch will generate $1bn in annual sales within a decade

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money podcast
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: The Swiss bank that crossed Scott Bessent
item.description: US pressure forced MBaer out of business, leaving critics questioning whether Swiss authorities had moved quickly enough

item.title: The pollution data they don’t print on your water bill
item.description: English water companies don’t publish numbers on how much waste they spill every year on your behalf — so I’ve estimated it

item.title: Trump trade chief urges US allies to pay more for critical minerals
item.description: Jamieson Greer says ‘security premium’ needed to counter reliance on Chinese supplies

item.title: New platform allows UK investors to hold crypto in Isas again
item.description: Start-up Stratiphy is the first to offer both crypto ETNs and Innovative Finance Isas

item.title: Is Norway really a war profiteer?
item.description: The country is facing pressure from its neighbours to share increased energy earnings to support Ukraine

item.title: Primark’s split is better fashionably late than never
item.description: Demerging retail creates two pure-play companies, both — given their scale — FTSE 100 constituents

item.title: OpenAI in talks to commit up to $1.5bn to private-equity joint venture
item.description: Start-up backing new company intended to help deploy AI within businesses owned by PE firms
